parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "profiles": ["{{ $('⚙️ Config & Route').first().json.scanTikTok.handle }}"],
  "resultsPerPage": {{ $('⚙️ Config & Route').first().json.maxPosts }},
  "shouldDownloadVideos": false,
  "shouldDownloadCovers": false,
  "shouldDownloadSubtitles": false
}

={
  "targetUrls": ["{{ $('⚙️ Config & Route').first().json.scanLinkedIn ? $('⚙️ Config & Route').first().json.scanLinkedIn.profileUrl : '' }}"],
  "maxPosts": {{ $('⚙️ Config & Route').first().json.maxPosts }},
  "includeReposts": false,
  "includeQuotePosts": true,
  "scrapeReactions": false,
  "scrapeComments": false
}
={{ $json.channels.map(c => ({ brand_id: $json.brand_id, channel: c.name, handle: (c.handle || '').replace(/^@/, ''), url: c.profileUrl, analysis_json: c, updated_at: $json.scanned_at })) }}

meta.notes: v11 — LinkedIn fully working. Added harvestapi/linkedin-company-posts ($2/1k, no cookies) + harvestapi/linkedin-company ($3/1k). New Merge LinkedIn node (n8n merge typeVersion 3, numberInputs=2) combines company+posts into one batch before Bundle LinkedIn — previously Bundle ran twice (once per upstream) producing posts:[] due to n8n batch semantics. Bundle LinkedIn separates items by shape (followerCount vs content/engagement). Tested swl-consulting-group: 140 followers, 30 posts, top 5 + tone analysis from Claude Haiku.
